prs.title: fix(react-start): align server-entry d.ts output with exports map
prs.body:
## Related Issue
Closes #7038

## Summary
- Fixes the `@tanstack/react-start/server-entry` declaration output path regression introduced with TS6 build config changes
- Adds a dedicated server-entry tsconfig with `rootDir: ./src/default-entry`
- Wires `vite.config.server-entry.ts` to that tsconfig so emitted declarations match package exports

## Root Cause
The server-entry build did not use a tsconfig with the correct `rootDir`, so declarations were emitted at:
- `dist/default-entry/esm/src/default-entry/server.d.ts` (actual)
instead of:
- `dist/default-entry/esm/server.d.ts` (export-mapped expected path)

## Validation
- `pnpm --filter @tanstack/react-start run clean && pnpm --filter @tanstack/react-start run build`
- Confirmed `dist/default-entry/esm/server.d.ts` exists
- Confirmed nested `dist/default-entry/esm/src/default-entry/server.d.ts` is no longer emitted

prs.title: docs: clarify nitro auto-imports with compatibilityVersion 5
prs.body:
### 🔗 Linked issue

Closes #34641

### 📚 Description

Document Nitro auto-import behavior when using `future.compatibilityVersion: 5`.

This adds migration guidance that Nitro auto-imports are disabled by default with compatibility v5 and explains two options:
- enable `experimental.nitroAutoImports: true`
- add explicit imports from `nitro/h3`

prs.title: Add monthly featured developer showcase
prs.body:
Highlights top GitHub developer each month based on engagement (followers, stars, repos, sponsors, activity). Features beautiful gradient banner with user stats and top languages. Auto-updates when running fetch.py.

**Current featured:** Linus Torvalds (engagement score: 74.97)

**What's included:**
- 🎯 Smart engagement scoring algorithm (40% followers, 30% stars, 15% repos, 10% sponsors, 5% recent activity)
- 🎨 Beautiful gradient banner with glassmorphism effects
- 📱 Fully responsive design (mobile, tablet, desktop)
- 🔄 Automatically updates when running `fetch.py`
- ⚡ Quick manual generation via `generate_featured.py`

Resolves #122

prs.title: feat(backend): enhance error logging in ComponentDatabaseHandler.getC…

labels.name: needs general test
labels.description: This is general testing, meaning that there is no org specific issue to check for

prs.body:
**Changes:**
- Add try-catch blocks to capture all exceptions in getComponent method
- Log component ID and error details for better traceability
- Log specific error when component is not found (404)
- Include full stack trace for unexpected exceptions
- Maintain existing exception throwing behavior

Resolves #3010

[//]: # (This program and the accompanying materials are made)
[//]: # (available under the terms of the Eclipse Public License 2.0)
[//]: # (which is available at https://www.eclipse.org/legal/epl-2.0/)
[//]: # (SPDX-License-Identifier: EPL-2.0)

## Summary

This PR enhances error logging in the `ComponentDatabaseHandler.getComponent()` method to improve debugging and observability when issues occur during component retrieval.

The current implementation throws exceptions when errors occur but doesn't log detailed information about the exception, making it difficult for developers and maintainers to debug issues.

**Issue**: #3010 - Enhance Error Logging in ComponentDatabaseHandler.getComponent

**Dependencies**: No new dependencies added. Uses existing SLF4J logger already present in the class.

### Suggest Reviewer
@GMishx @deo002

### How To Test?

The changes add logging only and do not modify the functional behavior. Testing can be done by:

1. **Component not found (404)**: Call `getComponent()` with invalid component ID. Expected: Error log shows "Component not found in database. Component ID: {id}"

2. **Database error**: Simulate database connectivity issue. Expected: Error log shows component ID and exception details with stack trace

3. **Successful retrieval**: Call with valid component ID and user. Expected: No error logs, component returned successfully (existing behavior unchanged)

### Checklist
Must:
- [x] All related issues are referenced in commit messages and in PR
- [x] No new dependencies added
- [x] Changes follow existing logging patterns in the project
- [x] No functional changes, only observability improvements

prs.title: feat(rest): enhance licenses API with missing fields and quick filter
prs.body:
Add shortname field to license response and implement quick filter functionality to improve API usability and search capabilities.

**Changes:**
- Include shortname in convertToEmbeddedLicense method
- Add luceneSearch parameter for license type filtering
- Support searchText parameter for quick filtering

Resolves #3407

[//]: # (This program and the accompanying materials are made)
[//]: # (available under the terms of the Eclipse Public License 2.0)
[//]: # (which is available at https://www.eclipse.org/legal/epl-2.0/)
[//]: # (SPDX-License-Identifier: EPL-2.0)

## Summary

This PR addresses issue #3407 by adding two missing features to the `/licenses` API:

1. **Missing Fields**: The `shortname` field was not being returned in license responses, even though it exists in the License model. This has been fixed by updating the `convertToEmbeddedLicense()` method in `RestControllerHelper`.

2. **Quick Filter Support**: Implemented lucene search capability for filtering licenses by license type, following the same pattern used in releases, projects, and components APIs.

**Issue**: #3407 - Missing fields in /licenses API and Quick Filter support

**Dependencies**: No new dependencies added. This PR uses existing service methods (`quickSearchLicenseType`) and follows established patterns.

### Suggest Reviewer
@deo002 @akshitjoshii @GMishx

### How To Test?

**Test 1: Verify shortname field is returned**
```bash
GET /api/licenses
```
Expected: Response now includes `shortname` field for each license

**Test 2: Quick filter without luceneSearch**
```bash
GET /api/licenses?page=0&size=20
```
Expected: Returns all licenses (existing behavior unchanged)

**Test 3: Quick filter with luceneSearch**
```bash
GET /api/licenses?luceneSearch=true&searchText=Apache
```
Expected: Returns only licenses with license type matching "Apache"

**Test 4: luceneSearch with empty searchText**
```bash
GET /api/licenses?luceneSearch=true
```
Expected: Returns all licenses (fallback to default behavior)

### Checklist
Must:
- [x] All related issues are referenced in commit messages and in PR
- [x] Changes follow existing code patterns in the project
- [x] No new compilation errors introduced
- [x] Pre-commit hooks passed

prs.title: docs: add comprehensive documentation for containSubset assertion
prs.body:
## Description

Adds comprehensive JSDoc documentation for the `containSubset` assertion that was added in #1664 but was never documented.

## Changes Made

### BDD Interface (`lib/chai/core/assertions.js`)
- Enhanced JSDoc for `.containSubset()` method with detailed descriptions
- Added multiple code examples showing:
  - Object subset matching
  - Array subset matching  
  - Nested structure comparisons
  - Function validators
  - Date object comparisons
  - Negation with `.not`
  - Custom error messages
- Documented the `subset` parameter
- Documented the optional `msg` parameter

### Assert Interface (`lib/chai/interface/assert.js`)
- Enhanced JSDoc for `assert.containsSubset()` 
- Enhanced JSDoc for `assert.doesNotContainSubset()`
- Added clear examples for both methods
- Documented function validator behavior
- Clarified the relationship between the two methods

## Testing
- All existing tests pass (510 passing)
- No new functionality added, only documentation improvements
- Lint checks pass

## Fixes
Closes #1738

## Additional Notes
This documentation follows the same format and style as other assertions in the codebase (e.g., `.include()`, `.equal()`, etc.). The documentation will be picked up by the documentation generation tools to update https://www.chaijs.com/api/bdd/

prs.title: feat(rest): add attachmentId query param to licenseFileList endpoint
prs.body:
[//]: # (This program and the accompanying materials are made)
[//]: # (available under the terms of the Eclipse Public License 2.0)
[//]: # (which is available at https://www.eclipse.org/legal/epl-2.0/)
[//]: # (SPDX-License-Identifier: EPL-2.0)

## Summary of Changes

This PR adds an optional `attachmentId` query parameter to the existing `/releases/{id}/licenseFileList` endpoint, enabling users to fetch the license file list for a specific attachment instead of only from the approved CLI attachment.

### Issue Reference
**Fixes #3472** - Need Endpoint to Fetch License File List of an Attachment of a Release

This solves the issue by:
- Modifying the existing `/releases/{id}/licenseFileList` endpoint to accept an optional `attachmentId` query parameter
- When `attachmentId` is provided, the endpoint returns the license file list for that specific attachment
- When `attachmentId` is NOT provided, the endpoint maintains the original behavior (returns license file list from approved CLI attachment)
- This enables the UI to show the license file list modal when clicking the info badge in the Release clearing details SPDX attachments table

### Dependencies
**No new dependencies added.** This change only modifies existing code to add the optional parameter.

### Changes Made
- **ReleaseController.java**: Added `attachmentId` as optional `@RequestParam` and updated OpenAPI documentation
- **Sw360ReleaseService.java**: Updated `getReleaseLicenseFileListInfo()` method to filter by specific attachment when `attachmentId` is provided

## Suggest Reviewer
@deo002 - Original issue author who can verify this implementation meets the requirements for the Release clearing details SPDX attachments table functionality

## How To Test?

### Testing Steps
1. **Test backward compatibility (without attachmentId parameter)**:
   ```bash
   GET /api/releases/{validReleaseId}/licenseFileList

prs.title: fix: enable decorations for structure: inline
prs.body:
- Invoke code transformer hooks for inline structures - Build synthetic code element to allow transformers like decorations to work - Add tests for single-line and multi-line decorations with inline structure Fixes #992 ### Description This PR fixes an issue where decorations don't work with `structure: inline` because the `transformerDecorations` transformer's `code` hook was not being invoked for inline structures. **What changed:** - Modified `tokensToHast` in `code-to-hast.ts` to invoke the `code` transformer hook for inline structures - For inline mode, a synthetic `` element is built from the root's children, transformers are applied, and the result is extracted back to inline format - This allows transformers like `transformerDecorations` to work correctly with inline structures while maintaining compatibility with all existing transformers **Implementation approach:** 1. Collect inline tokens and build a temporary line-based structure 2. Apply all `code` hook transformers (including decorations) 3. Flatten the structure back to inline format with `
` separators ### Linked Issues Fixes #992 ### Additional context - Added 2 new test cases with snapshots to verify single-line and multi-line decorations work correctly with `structure: inline` - All existing tests pass - The fix maintains backward compatibility - no breaking changes to the API or existing behavior

prs.title: feat: Add double arrow arrowhead option
prs.body:
## What

Adds a new double-sided arrow arrowhead option to the arrowhead picker, addressing the feature request in #10288.

## Why

Users requested the ability to have double-sided arrows (arrows with a middle line between the two outer arrow lines) for better visual representation in diagrams, especially for showing bidirectional relationships or enhanced emphasis.

## How

- Added `arrow_double` to the `Arrowhead` type definition
- Implemented rendering logic that draws three lines (two outer arrow lines + one middle line)
- Created the `ArrowheadArrowDoubleIcon` component for the UI
- Added the option to the arrowhead picker with keyboard shortcut `t`
- Added English localization string

## Changes

**Type definitions:**
- `packages/element/src/types.ts` - Added `arrow_double` to Arrowhead union type

**Rendering:**
- `packages/element/src/bounds.ts` - Added size and angle handling for arrow_double
- `packages/element/src/shape.ts` - Implemented three-line rendering (outer two + middle)

**UI:**
- `packages/excalidraw/components/icons.tsx` - Created ArrowheadArrowDoubleIcon component
- `packages/excalidraw/actions/actionProperties.tsx` - Added to arrowhead options with 't' shortcut

**Localization:**
- `packages/excalidraw/locales/en.json` - Added "arrowhead_arrow_double": "Double arrow"

## Testing

- ✅ Type checking passes
- ✅ No compilation errors
- ✅ Follows existing code patterns for arrowhead types
- ✅ Works with all arrow types (sharp, round, elbow)
- ✅ RTL support included

Closes #10288

prs.title: fix: re-export Duration type in intervalToDuration to fix TypeDoc documentation (#4059)
prs.body:
### Description

Fixes #4059 

This PR addresses the issue where the `Duration` interface documentation was not appearing in the TypeDoc-generated documentation when accessed through the `intervalToDuration` function page.

### Problem

When navigating to the Duration type from the `intervalToDuration` function documentation (https://date-fns.org/v4.1.0/docs/intervalToDuration#types/Duration/2523), the documentation page showed "No documentation available" despite the interface having proper JSDoc comments in `src/types.ts`.

### Solution

Added an explicit re-export of the `Duration` type in `src/intervalToDuration/index.ts`:

```typescript
// Re-export Duration type to ensure TypeDoc picks up its documentation
export type { Duration } from "../types.ts";
